description: Large mixed corpus — 50 turns covering identity, social network, hobbies, opinions, behavioral signals, and noise. Tests every probe type in one fixture: direct, multi-hop, temporal, aggregation, behavioral, vocabulary-mismatch, noise.
persona: Maya Lopez, design director with rich social network and behavioral patterns. Stable identity, evolving opinions, several friends with their own jobs and pets.

turns.content: I'm Maya Lopez, 38, design director at Linear. I live in Brooklyn, NY.
I've been at Linear for two and a half years. Before that I was at Figma for four years.
My partner is Kai. He's a documentary filmmaker, focused on environmental stories.
We have a tabby cat named Mango. She's seven years old, very chatty, and rules the apartment.
I'm pescatarian — fish and dairy yes, no other meat. Been this way since 2018.
My closest friend is Priya. She's a neurologist at NYU Langone. We've known each other since college.
Priya is married to Theo. Theo runs a coffee roastery in the East Village called Burr Coffee.
I have two main hobbies: ceramics and trail running. I've been throwing pots for about six years.
Trail running mostly in Prospect Park, sometimes upstate when I have a long weekend.
Another hobby — I read a lot. Mostly literary fiction. Just finished James by Percival Everett.
My sister Ines lives in Mexico City. She teaches sociology at UNAM.
Ines has a son, my nephew Dario. He's 7 and obsessed with dinosaurs.
My friend Lucia runs an indie magazine called Salt — design and culture, quarterly print.
Lucia and I used to work together at Figma. Now we collaborate on print projects sometimes.
Kai and I live in Crown Heights, near Franklin Avenue. We've been here three years.
I love Figma as a tool but I think they've gotten too feature-bloated since the Adobe acquisition fell through.
Honestly, the best design tool right now is still pen and paper for early ideation. Software is for production.
I think AI image generators are useful for moodboards but terrible for finished design work. The aesthetic is too generic.
When you explain something technical, please skip the metaphors. I want the actual mechanism, not an analogy.
I prefer concrete examples over abstract principles. Show me three real cases, not a framework.
Don't pad answers with caveats. If you're 80% sure, say so directly. I can handle uncertainty.
I work best in 2-hour focused blocks, then I need a break. Don't schedule me for 4-hour deep dives.
We're shipping a major Linear redesign in March. I'm leading the project, ten designers reporting to me.
The pressure on this redesign is intense — board-level visibility, three engineering teams blocked on it.
I'm trying to get my team to commit to less. We always overscope and miss.
Theo is hosting a coffee tasting at Burr next Friday. Inviting Priya, Lucia, me and Kai.
Mango has been weirdly clingy this week. Vet appointment tomorrow to check.
Vet says Mango is fine, just middle-aged-cat behavior. Relief.
Started bullet journaling for 2025. Feels productive but I'm not sure I'll keep it up past February.
Picked up a new hobby — taking a glassblowing class on Saturdays at UrbanGlass.
Linear redesign is now scoped at 60% of the original ambition. Team is happier, board less so.
Kai got accepted into the Sundance documentary lab. We're flying to Park City in March.
I'm changing my mind on AI-generated images. The newest models are good enough that I'm using them in client moodboards now.
Drinking too much coffee — Theo's stuff is dangerously good. Cutting back to two cups a day.
Ran 8 miles in Prospect Park this morning. Longest distance since November.
Lucia is launching the Spring issue of Salt next week. Doing a piece on AI in design — she asked me to write it.
Wrote 2000 words for Lucia's piece. Theme: AI is fine for ideation, dangerous for craft.
Mango is on a special diet now. Renal-support food. Vet wants to monitor her kidney values quarterly.
Reviewing 30 portfolios for the senior designer role we just opened. Five candidates moving to onsite interviews.
Glassblowing class is harder than ceramics. The molten material is unforgiving — you have one shot per pull.
Linear redesign is now in beta with 50 customer accounts. Early signal is positive.
Kai is in Park City for two weeks for the Sundance lab. I'm holding down Mango duty alone.
Just bought a new pottery wheel — Brent IE. Big upgrade from my old VL Whisper. Setting it up in the basement.
Priya is pregnant! Due in September. So thrilled for her and Theo.
Long run today — 12 miles. Marathon training is officially underway. Brooklyn Half in May.
Decided I want to learn Spanish properly this year. Mom's family is from Oaxaca and I can barely string a sentence together.
Honest opinion — most design Twitter discourse is just performance. Real design conversations happen in private slack groups.
Lucia's Spring Salt issue is out. My AI piece is the lead essay. Already getting weird DMs from AI bros.
Took Dario to the Natural History Museum dinosaur hall when Ines visited last weekend. He was in heaven.
Linear redesign shipped to all customers today. Two months later than planned but real proud of the team.

probes.query: where does the user live?
where does the user work?
what is the user's role?
what is the user's pet?
who is the user's partner?
what does the user eat?
what is the user's closest friend's job?
what business does the user's friend's husband run?
where does the user's sister teach?
who is pregnant?
what magazine does Lucia run?
where did the user used to work?
what was the user's old opinion on AI image generators?
list all the user's hobbies
what skills is the user developing?
how should I explain something technical to this user?
what communication style does this user prefer?
is the user under work pressure right now?
what does the user think about quantum computing?
what is the user's favorite tv show?
